description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a yellow and white color scheme, featuring two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically. The aircraft is equipped with large wooden floats instead of wheels, indicating it is a seaplane designed for water landings and takeoffs. The fuselage is streamlined and painted white, with a single open cockpit positioned just behind the upper wing. A wooden propeller is mounted at the nose, and the tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The wing struts and bracing wires are clearly visible, providing structural support between the wings and floats. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century seaplanes used for exploration or mail delivery.
description: A vintage yellow and white biplane seaplane with wooden floats, open cockpit, and a front-mounted wooden propeller.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual yellow wings, wooden propeller, and large floats beneath the fuselage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the stacked biplane wings, open cockpit, and the full length of the floats.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, twin floats, and the back of the cockpit and wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the biplane structure, float configuration, and tail surfaces.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed twin float landing gear for water operations.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing.
